facts.text: French traders founded Fort Rouillé in 1750, the site that later became Toronto’s Exhibition Grounds, before abandoning it in 1759 during the Seven Years' War.
Toronto’s abandoned Lower Bay station, the lower platform of Bay station, is a popular backdrop for subway scenes in movies and TV shows.
Toronto fields teams in every major North American league, from the NHL’s Maple Leafs to the MLB’s Blue Jays, the NBA’s Raptors, the CFL’s Argonauts, and MLS’s Toronto FC.
Because of its low production costs, Toronto has earned the nickname “Hollywood North,” ranking third in North America for film and television output after Los Angeles and New York.
A 19th‑century whisky factory built by Gooderham and Worts became Canada’s largest distillery, and its buildings are now a National Historic Site known as the Distillery District.
Toronto was selected as one of 16 North American cities to host matches for the 2026 FIFA World Cup, joining Montreal in the event’s Canadian leg.
